questions: You mentioned the Day 1 advisor tool result (+9 points) was an outlier — what specifically about that test case made it unrepresentative of how the tool would actually perform?

When you realized the Notion integration was becoming too complicated, you pivoted to building an abstract Connector interface instead — how much of that connector architecture did you already have planned versus discovering you needed it as you hit those OAuth and stdio problems?

The Streamlit rerun guard that prevents duplicate database writes only surfaced in production — what made you confident enough to deploy before catching that, and what would you do differently to catch those kinds of issues earlier?
